name: Resetable

unions.name: Resetable
unions.comment:
Wraps a type in a Resetable.
You can construct a `Resetable` using `init`.

values.name: changed
values.comment:
Check if the value has changed.

    Resetable.init "hello"
        |> Resetable.changed
    --> False

    Resetable.init "hello"
        |> Resetable.update "world"
        |> Resetable.changed
    --> True

values.name: init
values.comment:
Creates a Resetable and initializes its value.

    Resetable.init "a"

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map a resetable value.
This allows you to change the value without loosing its original value.

    Resetable.init "hello"
        |> Resetable.map String.toUpper
        |> Resetable.value
    --> "HELLO"

values.name: reset
values.comment:
Reset a Resetable to its original value.

    Resetable.init "hello"
        |> Resetable.map String.toUpper
        |> Resetable.reset
        |> Resetable.value
    --> "hello"

values.name: update
values.comment:
Update a resetable value.
This allows you to change the value without loosing its original value.

    Resetable.init "hello"
        |> Resetable.update "world"
        |> Resetable.value
    --> "world"

values.name: value
values.comment:
Get the current value of a Resetable.

    Resetable.init "a"
        |> Resetable.value
    --> "a"
